content.problemStatement:
An array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] is said to be good when the following condition holds:


For every pair of subsets XXX and YYY of indices in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N], such that ∣X∣<∣Y∣|X| < |Y|∣X∣<∣Y∣, it holds that the sum of elements in XXX is **strictly smaller** than the sum of elements in YYY.

More formally, it should hold that ∑i∈XAi<∑i∈YAi\sum_{i \in X}A_i < \sum_{i \in Y}A_i∑i∈X​Ai​<∑i∈Y​Ai​.
For example, [2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3] is a good array and we can verify this condition. But, [2,2,4][2, 2, 4][2,2,4] is not because consider X={3}X = \{3\}X={3} and Y={1,2}Y = \{1, 2\}Y={1,2}. ∑i∈XAi=4\sum_{i \in X}A_i = 4∑i∈X​Ai​=4 and ∑i∈YAi=2+2=4\sum_{i \in Y}A_i = 2 + 2 = 4∑i∈Y​Ai​=2+2=4. Both have the same sum 444 despite XXX having fewer elements, and this contradicts the condition.

You are given an array AAA of NNN integers.

Find the number of non-empty subsequences of the array AAA which are good.

A subsequence is an array formed by deleting some (possibly none) elements from the array, but without changing the order of the remaining elements. Note that there are 2N−12^N - 12N−1 possible non-empty subsequences of an array with NNN integers.

Note: For a set SSS, ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ denotes the cardinality or size of the set SSS.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
Output the number of good subsequences of AAA.
constraints: **Subtask 4 (13 points):** There are at most 333 distinct values in the array AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 777 subsequences as enlisted - [1],[1],[2],[1,1],[1,2],[1,2][1], [1], [2], [1, 1], [1, 2], [1, 2][1],[1],[2],[1,1],[1,2],[1,2] and [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2]. Note that some of them appear multiple times.
All of them except [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2] are good, and hence, there are 666 good subsequences.
